title: VFE = Complexity - Accuracy

runtime.expected_outcome: Every belief sweep prints F = complexity - accuracy; both terms individually plotted, their signs interpretable.

math.latex: F[q] = \mathbb{E}_{q(s)}[\ln q(s) - \ln p(o, s)] = D_{KL}[q(s) \| p(s)] - \mathbb{E}_{q(s)}[\ln p(o|s)]

symbols.F[q]: variational free energy at a posterior q

audiences.kid: Free energy is like a score where you lose points for changing your mind too much (complexity) and lose points for being surprised (inaccuracy).
audiences.real: F decomposes cleanly into KL-from-prior (how much the posterior moved) and expected log-likelihood (how well it explains the data). Minimising F balances the two.
audiences.equation: F = D_KL[q||p] - E_q[ln p(o|s)]. First term grows with belief updates; second term grows as accuracy drops.
audiences.derivation: From F = E_q[ln q - ln p(o,s)] and p(o,s) = p(o|s) p(s), split into E_q[ln q - ln p(s)] (KL) and -E_q[ln p(o|s)] (neg accuracy). Standard variational decomposition (Jordan et al. 1999).

orchestrate.objective: Decompose F into complexity and accuracy and visualise the tradeoff across a belief sweep.
orchestrate.role: Variational-calculus tutor; grounds the free-energy minimisation intuition in two visible quantities.
orchestrate.context: tiny_open_goal with 1 observation; F is a scalar plotted against sweep iteration.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 4 & App B.
Jordan, Ghahramani, Jaakkola & Saul (1999), Introduction to variational methods.
